title: The Lapahn Witness Luffy’s Resolve
description: On pages 13–16, the lapahn witness luffy’s resolve as after Wapol blocks the way, Luffy keeps carrying his injured friends up the mountain despite the cold and attacks.
scene: The Lapahn Witness Luffy’s Resolve. After Wapol blocks the way, Luffy keeps carrying his injured friends up the mountain despite the cold and attacks. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Hang in there! It'll be just a little bit longer...

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: These rabbits seriously thought they could go against a king!?

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Hmph... though to be honest, I didn't expect fighting a bunch of rabbits would force me to use my real power.

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: But they are drum island's most violent animals for a reason, your majesty.

summary: This part centers on the lapahn witness luffy’s resolve, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
